title: Fair 4-way Show-o vs MMaDA-8B on Hard64 under ONE identical clean Gemini judge

judge.rubric: strict prompt-following single-image clean judge, PASS when score>=0.5
judge.note: Identical rubric/口径 across ALL four arms (this is the apples-to-apples comparison the user asked for).

mmada.note: MMaDA high-quality default = official eval config stage3_512_cot (20 steps / gs=5). README inference example uses 15/3.5; both documented, 20/5 chosen as the HQ default.

selector.showo_arm: Qwen3.5-9B (qwen_vl) original coarse ASCR (4x4 -> dilation -> 32x32 reopen), single-process .venv-qwen36
selector.mmada_arm: Qwen3.5-9B (qwen_vl) original coarse ASCR, two-process file-IPC pipeline (MMaDA .venv-mmada + Qwen server .venv-qwen36)

mmada_qwen_coarse_hq.note: single-image -1 is within Gemini judge noise; debiased pairwise vs baseline is 3 wins / 0 losses (ASCR never regresses).

conclusions: Under ONE identical clean Gemini rubric, Show-o (1.5B) baseline = 73.4% clearly beats MMaDA-8B HQ baseline = 57.8%; cross-model debiased pairwise = Show-o 26 wins / 5 / 33 ties (83.9% decisive).

Show-o's 73.4% was NOT merely a rubric artifact: even at MMaDA's high-quality default (20 steps / gs=5) and with the same strong external Qwen-9B coarse selector, Show-o still decisively outperforms MMaDA-8B on this compositional Hard64 set (Show-o+Qwen 78.1% vs MMaDA+Qwen 56.2%; pairwise Show-o 29 / 3 / 32, 90.6% decisive).
Coarse ASCR with Qwen-9B helps Show-o (+4.7pp clean, 5/0/59 pairwise) and never regresses MMaDA (debiased 3/0/61; the -1.6pp single-image clean is judge noise).

MMaDA's high-quality default generation_timesteps = 20 (gs=5) per its official eval config stage3_512_cot; the README inference example uses 15/3.5. We used 20/5 as the HQ default, so MMaDA was given its best-quality setting in this fair comparison.
